subject_output:
# New Recipe: `cooked_fish`

Below is the new recipe file. It is the only file that needs to be added; it conforms to the recipe schema documented in `gdd/content/recipes.md` (id / tier / station / tool / inputs / outputs / craft_time, plus an embedded `item` block declaring the produced food item's properties — same pattern used by the other consumable-producing recipes like `berry_mash` and `boiled_water`).

**File:** `content/recipes/cooked_fish.yaml`

```yaml
# Cooked fish — tier-2 cooking recipe.
# Player roasts a raw_fish at the campfire into a more nutritious cooked_fish.
# Gated behind the wooden axe tier (matches the rest of the t2 chain that
# unlocks once the player has felled their first tree and built a sawhorse).

id: cooked_fish
name: Cooked Fish
tier: 2

# Crafting requirements
station: campfire           # must be performed at a built campfire
tool_required: wooden_axe   # tier-2 gating tool (per the recipe-tree spec)

inputs:
  - item: raw_fish
    quantity: 1

outputs:
  - item: cooked_fish
    quantity: 1

# ~30 in-game minutes. One in-game day is ~6 minutes of wall-clock split
# across 4 phases (~90 in-game min per phase), so this is roughly a third
# of a phase — i.e. about 1 wall-clock minute of crafting time.
craft_time_minutes: 30

# Item definition for the produced good. cooked_fish is a food item; eating
# it refills 6 hunger. No thirst effect, no health effect, not stackable
# beyond the default food stack size (inherits engine default).
item:
  id: cooked_fish
  name: Cooked Fish
  type: food
  description: >
    A fillet of fish, roasted over the campfire until it flakes. Keeps
    longer than raw fish and fills you up more.
  effects:
    hunger_restore: 6
  consumed_on_use: true

# Notes for designers (ignored by the loader):
# - Sits in the same tier-2 "campfire cooking" cluster as berry_mash.
# - Encourages routing the player past a tidepool (raw_fish source) on
#   Day 2–3 once the wooden axe + campfire are online, which is the
#   pre-registered intended path for avoiding the Day-4 food crash.
```

### Why these values

- **`tier: 2` / `tool_required: wooden_axe`** — matches the prompt's "tier-2 (requires the wooden axe as the gating tool tier)". Per the design pillars, tier gating is what makes "the graph is the game" legible.
- **`station: campfire`** — explicit in the prompt ("at a campfire").
- **`inputs` / `outputs`** — 1 → 1 conversion, as specified.
- **`craft_time_minutes: 30`** — the prompt's "about 30 in-game minutes." With ~6 wall-clock minutes per in-game day (~24 in-game hours), 30 in-game minutes ≈ 1.25 wall-clock seconds of compressed time, which the loader resolves against the day clock; consistent with other cooking recipes.
- **`item.effects.hunger_restore: 6`** — directly from the prompt. Placed under the embedded `item` block so this single file is self-contained (no edits required to a separate items registry, per the "one new file" constraint).
- **`consumed_on_use: true`** — standard for food items so the eat action removes it from inventory.

# New Recipe: `cooked_fish`

Below is the new recipe file. It is the only file that needs to be added; it conforms to the recipe schema documented in `gdd/content/recipes.md` (id / tier / station / tool / inputs / outputs / craft_time, plus an embedded `item` block declaring the produced food item's properties — same pattern used by the other consumable-producing recipes like `berry_mash` and `boiled_water`).

**File:** `content/recipes/cooked_fish.yaml`

```yaml
# Cooked fish — tier-2 cooking recipe.
# Player roasts a raw_fish at the campfire into a more nutritious cooked_fish.
# Gated behind the wooden axe tier (matches the rest of the t2 chain that
# unlocks once the player has felled their first tree and built a sawhorse).

id: cooked_fish
name: Cooked Fish
tier: 2

# Crafting requirements
station: campfire           # must be performed at a built campfire
tool_required: wooden_axe   # tier-2 gating tool (per the recipe-tree spec)

inputs:
  - item: raw_fish
    quantity: 1

outputs:
  - item: cooked_fish
    quantity: 1

# ~30 in-game minutes. One in-game day is ~6 minutes of wall-clock split
# across 4 phases (~90 in-game min per phase), so this is roughly a third
# of a phase — i.e. about 1 wall-clock minute of crafting time.
craft_time_minutes: 30

# Item definition for the produced good. cooked_fish is a food item; eating
# it refills 6 hunger. No thirst effect, no health effect, not stackable
# beyond the default food stack size (inherits engine default).
item:
  id: cooked_fish
  name: Cooked Fish
  type: food
  description: >
    A fillet of fish, roasted over the campfire until it flakes. Keeps
    longer than raw fish and fills you up more.
  effects:
    hunger_restore: 6
  consumed_on_use: true

# Notes for designers (ignored by the loader):
# - Sits in the same tier-2 "campfire cooking" cluster as berry_mash.
# - Encourages routing the player past a tidepool (raw_fish source) on
#   Day 2–3 once the wooden axe + campfire are online, which is the
#   pre-registered intended path for avoiding the Day-4 food crash.
```

Why these values

- **`tier: 2` / `tool_required: wooden_axe`** — matches the prompt's "tier-2 (requires the wooden axe as the gating tool tier)". Per the design pillars, tier gating is what makes "the graph is the game" legible.
- **`station: campfire`** — explicit in the prompt ("at a campfire").
- **`inputs` / `outputs`** — 1 → 1 conversion, as specified.
- **`craft_time_minutes: 30`** — the prompt's "about 30 in-game minutes." With ~6 wall-clock minutes per in-game day (~24 in-game hours), 30 in-game minutes ≈ 1.25 wall-clock seconds of compressed time, which the loader resolves against the day clock; consistent with other cooking recipes.
- **`item.effects.hunger_restore: 6`** — directly from the prompt. Placed under the embedded `item` block so this single file is self-contained (no edits required to a separate items registry, per the "one new file" constraint).
- **`consumed_on_use: true`** — standard for food items so the eat action removes it from inventory.

intent_score.rationale: The output perfectly matches the task brief and adheres to the design brief's logic. It correctly implements the 1:1 ratio, the 6-hunger refill, the campfire station, and the tier-2 wooden axe requirement. The craft time of 30 in-game minutes is correctly interpreted in the context of the design brief's day rhythm.
